_description: Human-verified labels for dense-count batch 2 (seeds 78000-78011, 1024x1024 SDXL Turbo). Density-descriptor prompts with no numeric counts. Labeled by Claude on 2026-04-16.
_finding_v2: SDXL doesn't just ignore 'exactly N' — it also INVERTS density adjectives. Prompts for 'sparse,' 'handful,' 'otherwise empty,' and 'half full' all produced dense/packed scenes. The model has a strong prior toward filling the frame with the subject. Only 'dense' and 'packed' prompts produce the expected density. Implication: SDXL alone cannot be used to generate low-density or exact-count test images — those need programmatic composition.

images.notes: Prompt wanted sparse, got medium. Courtyard with ~32 people scattered. Clean lighting, good separation between figures. Relabel as 'medium' and keep.
Ring-shaped crowd in plaza, very tightly packed. Hundreds of people in concentric circles. Good high-count candidate.

Massive crowd with an awning dividing the frame. Prompt delivered. Extreme-count image — useful for testing the upper end of VLM counting capability where no model will get exact numbers but can still rank by magnitude.
Prompt wanted sparse cluster, got a warehouse full. Regular grid, no stacking, good lighting. Relabel as high-count grid and keep.
Regular grid of brown boxes on warehouse floor. Clean lighting, distinct separation. Best 'countable grid' image in the batch — rows and columns are visible which helps a labeler.
Dry-color forest canopy with NO clear ground visible. Prompt inverted. Duplicate of trees_dense theme. Reject as redundant — trees_dense is better.
Mature forest canopy. Canopies merge at edges — the boundary between adjacent trees is ambiguous even to a human. Good test for VLM handling of uncertain instance boundaries.
5 shelves of bottles, mixed green and clear glass. Two distinct bottle types — useful for testing class-conditional counting ('how many green bottles?').
Bicycle icons tiled as wallpaper, not a 'small grid.' But icons are uniform and countable. Relabel and keep — 28 bicycles is a reasonable medium test.
Leaf icons scattered on white. Less dense than prompted, more natural-looking. Good counting test at ~25 instances.
Dense parking lot with color-varied cars (white/black/red cars as potential class-filter targets). Useful for extreme-count AND class-conditional counting.

EIGHT folding chairs arranged in a loose 3-row cluster on wood floor. GROUND TRUTH IS RELIABLE at this count. The first image in the entire smoke batch where a human-verified exact count is trivial. Pattern: simple subjects in quantities that resemble natural scene counts work.
